messages.text: Ingredients ratio truff  test check update
Tempering chart test check update temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Check weight test check update 


The weights of the final product look good; they fall within the limits required by the product specifications.

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Flow wrap test check update 


The flow wrap is well-sealed along the sides, and the "best by" date is legible and clearly printed in the correct location; the appearance is acceptable but could be improved, and the team leader has already been notified regarding these improvements.
Maintenance is being called because the jaws have separated from the bands, causing the chocolate to spill downwards.
The enrober's screen indicates that the blowers and the belt are turned off, even though everything is actually running.
Cavanna 2 needed the outfeed belt moved closer/tightened down to prevent chocolates from dropping under the outfeed conveyor.
Maintenance was called, and the problem was resolved.
Doing a 25 min pause to run 35 bins of accumulation. Maintenance has been notified of TL going down incase adjustments are needed
The OPL is located near the guillotine screen, providing a good view.
The M6 ​​belt was inspected again—a procedure referred to as maintenance—and we are currently waiting for the technician to arrive.
Preston Elmer Nathan Ruiz  it rejecting everything off sent to cavanna 1
We are still waiting for maintenance to center the M6 ​​belt; it is causing issues with product accumulation.

Rich Brierley,

We have a break on production at L1 so Scott is on his way to help.
Thanks he is here  on molding line 1 and truffle line
The chocolate has shifted position and is causing accumulation issues.

Capture test

Nathan Gevenois, Angel Cabrera

What is the target % of oil?

I think it is supposed to be 22% or 23%
we Can do another test to be on target
Let’s get to 22% and even slightly above I am fine with
M6 belt running correctly.

TruffleLine Enrober did we adjust the oil percentage like we spoke about earlier? 
Nathan Marcos Montano
New capture test
Metal shavings found on md reject section. It doesn’t seem like it’s touching the belt we’re going to stop so maintenance can work on it
No metal hits have been recorded today. Seems like the metal shaving is dropping on the exterior part where there’s no food contact.
Do you want us to stop or keep going?
Looks like the end of the run is almost to tunnel 4. We can keep going because the metal shaving isn’t touching where the product is
Maintenance finished. Production cleaned the metal detector bin and belts and around the bin
How is this work going Raoul Lubain?
Maintenance is working on changing M6 belt roller
Sorry I thought I had sent a message. But this has been finished and is ready to run. We also tracked the belt.
Our Lead going home sick. We are unable to the line.
We have 1st Shift kitchen operator coming in early to help
M5 belt wont turn on maintenance is currently working to fix the issue
Checking the belts function is on the SU checklist, correct? Step 3?
The M5 belt was making contact with the M6 causing the M5 motor to trip. Made adjustments to the M5 belt to give it some breathing room and is running fine now.
Tempering chart test check update temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.
We have 40 bins of accumulation. We will make a 40 min pause to pass it
The control for Cavanna 4 is uneven; I spoke to the supervisor, and he told me that was how he received it.
Sorry, I forgot to post on it at SOS

Checking 7am
51 pieces

Rich Brierley, Frederick

Please prioritize Cavanna 0 on ML1

At steady state we're more or less okay. When we have pauses for infeed supply we're noticing more issues. Working on balancing that speed with adjustments to the jaws position as well

Just walked out 5min ago

Replaced the tooth on the Cavanna it's back up and running

The product continues to come out with open seals. Nathan and Shawn are on the line working to resolve the issue.
No longer really getting chocolate cut by jaws, just having incomplete seals on corner
Still working on adjustments. What scott recommended has not resolved issue
The product continues to come out with open seals. Nathan is on the line working to resolve the issue. The product will be put on hold
Glycol is good
Metal detector belt, chain and belt of imperial, Demolding and c6 belt are running correctly

Thank you!!

Is Cavanna Scott here?

Chad Stone Rich Brierley Robert Villarreal Nathan Gevenois Raoul Lubain Alan Greer Marcos Montano after speaking with Robert there's no need to reduce Mass Pump speed when running at 5mpm unless we are going to run 8 hours or longer at 5mpm. If we are having cavanna issues reduce MPM to 5 and run the Bosch until cavanna can be fixed then increase speed back to 15-16 mpm
Cavanna machine stopped,  maintenance is working on it.

No he is not. 

I was going to have him come by tomorrow.
Do yall need him tonight Marcos Montano?
Although there’s a noticeable improvement, I’m still encountering open flow wraps. We’ll continue monitoring the situation and placing pallets on hold.
Are we not sorting the bins like we were on 1st shift Nathan Gevenois, Marcos Montano?
Yeah we are

Angel Cabrera, Preston Tregaskis,

Why are we putting pallets on hold if we are sorting during production?
It’s starting to look better. Maintenance is there and has worked on it
It’s not 100% inspection or sorting. Few cases get missed. Line is running way too fast for each case to be inspected. All if first shift product went on hold as well
We inspected cases on a pallet and the defect is less than 1%. Team will continue sorting and monitoring. QA team will not need to place pallets on hold moving forward
Metal detector belt, chain and belt of imperial, Demolding and c6 belt are running correctly

Thank you for digging into that. Let's keep a close eye on it, but we long as we're in the 1-3% defect range, I feel good about the inspections being done. If we start seeing issues, reach out to me and we can talk through it.
Yeah I feel confident we’re in that range now 👍 team is aware to continue monitoring
Marcos Montano Nathan Gevenois please keep an eye on this. I'm glad your teams are handling it, but we can't let the inspections slip.

Good evening Marcos Montano,

Can you give me a call?

I got an idea for spending the Feastables coming off the Cavanna.

Angel Cabrera?
I think it a good concept, we had to use more EEs to run it but i think we will be able identify open easier

Hi Team,

There was an issue with the Sucrotherm motor overload. Chris Givens and Chris Jeffstold me that the maximum motor load is 22 amps. The overload appears to be caused by the temperature setting being too low at 130°F.

I spoke with Nathan Topham and we increased the Sucrotherm temperature to 145°F, and the motor load is now 17.5  amps.

I also lowered the pipe temperature from 172°F to 165°F.

Please keep an eye on the drip at the collection tray/reservoir.

Here are some pictures.

Ok.  For now remove it with my approval understanding that we cannot trace water.

Miriah Manzanares Will work on tracing water so we can do it on the next run.

I’ll send an email as well stating this
Thank you, Curt! Paula Pena Go ahead with the creation of the caramel labels.

While the line is paused, I noticed this symbol on the screen for the depositor area. In the past, this has indicated a glitch in the software which prevents us from making any adjustments to the line speed or other settings. This is just a heads-up, as I can't confirm that this is the case until we resume the line. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Also, the wrong recipe is being used. Nathan Topham Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis

Good afternoon. The line is currently stopped due to issues with the metal detector; mechanics are working to resolve the problem. A check of the cards was performed, and all were successfully rejected.
The MD and C2 are good now William Beattie Dakota Adair
The hard reset on the Sollich panel was successful
Temper now
Maintenance cycled the power to reset the system, and all appears well again. We are preparing to resume production. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Depositing now. Caramel looks good.
The valve on the East side was closed, preventing chocolate from filling the trough completely. Was there a reason for this, Nathan Topham?
The vibrating trough was not installed to catch the macadamias
Yes
Curt WillisDakota Adair let's document, in the batch time spreadsheet, that water was used in each batch. That way we can go back and ensure that water was added to each batch.

Also the recipe does as for water if 43 pounds per batch

We noticed that the "sanitizing log", or whatever it was, is no longer in the drain. Was this removed intentionally? Or do we need a replacement? Charity Young Jorel Gourdin
We have many pieces of caramel that They return to the main band
If this is the 3x a shift sanitation for enrober drain, that is not needed any longer.
This is causing us to have dobles pieces; can we please address this? William Beattie
We need one person on each side who can take this caramel out or close  the separation table
No longer need to do extra sanitizer
Yes, I'll have the line lead check the gaps of the separating tables.

My knowledge on the subject is limited, but from what I understand, there was essentially a "urinal-cake", except it was designed to be used in our floor-drain...? I apologize I don't have a better description for the thing.

The 3 x a shift is no longer needed.

The other object in the drain is used to dissolve grease
Did you check the end of the drain on the opposite side?
We need someone at the start of the line to remove the candy that gets sent back; we have a lot of doubles and triples at the end.
Yes, it's not there.

If we close the gaps any more, we send too many nuts through the enrober and to the jar area. It is impossible to prevent caramel from falling onto the return conveyors. We do need at least one person assign3d to removing caramel from the bed before the shaker. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Thanks! They are de-greasers. We just wanted to try it out. Maybe better suited for the kitchens!
We are noticing a lot of loose macadamia nuts on the belt, and the jars are overfilled; please, we need to improve this.
Thank you for your attention. Alfonsina Gil. Can you please look into this? William Beattie Conner Boyd

I am informing the operator.
We've resumed depositing. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis
We've paused depositing in order to address the caramel issue. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis

I worked on it for an hour, not sure why it's no longer printing in it's normal spot. Is the information (password etc.) about the new printer for the cartons available anywhere? I don't recall any information given about the 23xx series

Just to add a little more detail to the situation: The line lead reported that the printer wasn't working at 9:40pm. Maintenance was called, and they discovered that a cable was somehow disconnected. They've reattached the cable, and it is now printing but not in the correct position. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis

I just got off the phone with Troy Baxter, we tried a few things but can't get the printer to print on the South side of the trays. He said he'll come in to assist in person. In the meantime, we have several pallets which will need to be reworked. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair Curt Willis

Sorry I forgot to post about this. Talking to Felix when I got in today. He noticed that the problem only happens with the double stacking when the WDS would slow down. So at the start of the shift we turned off the automatic slow down of the WDS and had the person at the discharge watch the fill level and stop and start the WDS manually. Not being able to adjust speed is causing high scrap on the line.
One of our operators forgot and slowed down the machine due to TG5 being down for a significant amount of time and it caused the double stacking.
With the box erector We have the same amount of people tonight of 18. We are using the 3 people to check the product instead of helping open boxes.
All these pauses are causing a lot of scrap we are over 3000lbs tonight. Do we want 3rd shift to keep running.

We are seeing a lot of flow-wrapped chocolates with poor end-sealing; the packaging isn't staying closed, leaving the chocolate exposed—essentially, the outer ends are open.

Everyone,

While Scott was at the line with Aubrey and our Bequet customer, he shared that Quality is seeing micros coming from the Twist Wrap line and believes glove and hand hygiene may be a contributing factor. Scott stressed that this is important feedback, especially since it aligns with observations shared by the FDA this week.

Scott asked me to help remind our teams  the following:
-Wear clean gloves before touching the line or any product-contact surfaces.
-If you leave the line, wash or sanitize your hands and put on a new pair of gloves before touching the line again.
-Change gloves frequently to maintain GMP compliance.

Thank you for your support.
